name: Physics-R1
description: Physics-R1: An audited visual physics RL training corpus and held-out olympiad evaluation. This Croissant aggregates the four Hugging Face datasets that ship with the NeurIPS 2026 Datasets & Benchmarks submission. Lineage: 14,294 pre-audit raw master corpus -> 7,236 construction-audit-clean candidate (Stage-1 5-gram Jaccard >= 0.4 union Stage-2 mxbai-embed-large cosine >= 0.85, construction-audit set: eval_mini, eval_full, PhysOlym-A, PhyX-test, MMMU-Pro Physics, OlympiadBench-Physics-EN) -> 6,432 audit-clean PhysCorp-A v2 after a joint re-audit pass against PhysReason-full and PhysUniBench-en (which were not in the construction-audit set) dropped 804 leaking records (PhysReason-full 540, PhysUniBench-en 186 dominant) -> 2,268 closed-form RL pool PhysR1Corp via three sequential cleanup passes from a 2,433-record candidate: (i) one PR-full duplicate at cos=0.984 dropped, (ii) 87 records dropped against MMMU-Pro Physics under joint Stage-1 OR Stage-2 thresholds, (iii) 78 records dropped at Stage-2 cos>=0.85 against PhyX-mini (69 near-duplicate variants, top cos 0.93) and PhysUniBench-en (9 template duplicates), with 19 remaining Stage-2 hits across PhysOlym-A/MMMU-Pro/OlymBench/PhysReason kept as same-topic neighbors (top cos <= 0.87). Held-out evaluation: 500-record PhysOlym-A (99.8% novel-source). Per-eval audit results vs PhysR1Corp 2,268 (Stage-1 / Stage-2 / Stage-3 close-duplicates): OlympiadBench-Physics 0/4/0, PhyX-mini 0/0/0, MMMU-Pro Physics 0/1/0, PhysOlym-A 0/3/0, PhysReason-full 0/11/0, PhysUniBench-en 0/0/0 (all 19 remaining Stage-2 hits are same-topic neighbors at top cos <= 0.87). Code, audit pipeline, training recipe, eval harness, and Sonnet-as-judge scripts are released at https://github.com/shanyang-me/physics-r1-neurips2026.

distribution.name: physcorp_pre_audit
distribution.description: 14,294-record pre-audit raw master corpus aggregating nine source families before the two-stage contamination audit. Released so users can re-run the audit at different thresholds or against new external benchmarks.

distribution.name: physcorp_a
distribution.description: 6,432-record audit-clean PhysCorp-A v2. Subset of the 14,294 pre-audit pool that (i) survives the construction-audit Stage-1 (5-gram Jaccard >= 0.4) and Stage-2 (mxbai-embed-large-v1 cosine >= 0.85) audits against eval_mini, eval_full, PhysOlym-A, PhyX-test, MMMU-Pro Physics, and OlympiadBench-Physics-EN (yielding a 7,236-record candidate), and then (ii) survives a joint re-audit pass against PhysReason-full and PhysUniBench-en (which were not in the construction-audit set) at the same thresholds, which dropped 804 additional records dominated by PhysReason-full (540) and PhysUniBench-en (186). The released v2 is joint-clean (0/0 by construction) at the joint operating thresholds against all six paper-canonical evals.

distribution.name: physr1corp
distribution.description: 2,268-record closed-form RL training pool. Carve-out of PhysCorp-A v2 (6,432-record audited corpus) keeping only numeric / MCQ-gradable items (binary correctness reward is defined on closed-form gold). Three decontamination passes: (1) one record dropped where Stage-2 mxbai cosine >= 0.85 flagged near-text duplication against PhysReason-full (lightning-charge, cos=0.984); (2) 87 records dropped against MMMU-Pro Physics under joint Stage-1 (J>=0.4) OR Stage-2 (cos>=0.85) audit, covering 52/60 = 86.7% of MMMU-Pro; (3) 78 records dropped against PhyX-mini (69) and PhysUniBench-en (9 template duplicates) at Stage-2 cos>=0.85 (top cos ~ 0.93 confirmed real physics-problem variants by manual inspection). 18 remaining Stage-2 hits across PhysOlym-A (3), MMMU-Pro (1), OlymBench-Phys (4), PhysReason-full (10) are topic-similarity false positives (top cos <= 0.87) and kept in pool. This is the pool used for Physics-R1 RL training in Table 2.

distribution.name: PhysOlym-A
distribution.description: 500-problem 99.8%-novel-source open-ended olympiad held-out evaluation. Composed of ~200 problems from Kevin Zhou's olympiad handouts, ~136 from the Estonian Physics Olympiad (with native 1-10 difficulty labels and 201-problem bilingual EN/ET subset), ~85 from IPhO/NBPhO/EuPhO, ~79 from APhO/USAPhO/INPhO. The single 0.2% non-novel record is an EuPhO 2020 problem also present in OlympiadBench-Physics.

recordSet.name: corpus_record
recordSet.description: One physics problem in the training-corpus parquets (physcorp_pre_audit, physcorp_a). Schema is shared across the audited and pre-audit pools.

field.name: source
field.description: Source family identifier (ugphysics, openstax, physics_se, mmmu, physreason, efo, zhou, ipho_nbpho_eupho, apho_usapho_inpho).

field.name: messages
field.description: JSON-serialized array of {role, content} chat messages for the problem statement; multimodal records embed <image> markers.

field.name: solution
field.description: Reference solution / gold answer (string or JSON-serialized object for multi-part).

field.name: metadata
field.description: JSON-serialized metadata: {concept, difficulty, problem-type, math-level, modality, language, license, expected-solution-length, audit-passed, source-family, native-difficulty, ...}.

recordSet.name: rl_record
recordSet.description: One RL training record in the verl-compatible PhysR1Corp parquet. Closed-form prompts (numeric or MCQ-gradable) with binary correctness rewards.

field.name: prompt
field.description: List of {role, content} chat messages; multimodal prompts embed <image> markers aligned with the images field.

field.name: images
field.description: List of {image_url} entries (file:// paths in the original verl format).

recordSet.name: olympiad_record
recordSet.description: One held-out olympiad problem in PhysOlym-A. Open-ended physics-olympiad problems from Estonian PhO, Kevin Zhou's handouts, and seven international olympiads.

field.name: source
field.description: Source family: kevin_zhou, estonian_olympiad, ipho_nbpho_eupho, apho_usapho_inpho.

field.name: messages
field.description: JSON-serialized array of {role, content} chat messages for the problem statement.

field.name: metadata
field.description: Metadata including native difficulty (Estonian 1-10, Zhou 1-5 + advanced [A] flag), language (en, et, en-et), and license.

rai:dataCollection: Records aggregated from nine source families (Sept 2024 - Mar 2026): UGPhysics, OpenStax College/University Physics, Physics Stack Exchange, MMMU+o1-CoT seed, PhysReason, Estonian Physics Olympiad (CC BY-NC 4.0, confirmed in writing with EFO president 2026-04-22), Kevin Zhou's olympiad handouts (CC BY-NC 4.0, confirmed by author 2026-05-03), IPhO/NBPhO/EuPhO scrape, APhO/USAPhO/INPhO/IYPT scrape. Per-source URLs, scrape dates, and license strings are documented in Appendix A of the paper.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Sonnet 4.5 batch annotation with eight-field schema (concept, difficulty, problem-type, expected-solution-length, math-level, modality, language, license). 3,900 records carry full annotation; the remainder carry source-native labels merged into the same schema (~31,000 total label values).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Three-stage contamination audit applied pairwise across PhyX, MMMU-Pro Physics, OlympiadBench-Physics, eval_mini, eval_full, and PhysOlym-A. Stage 1: 5-gram shingle Jaccard >= 0.4. Stage 2: mxbai-embed-large-v1 normalized embedding cosine >= 0.85. Stage 3: Haiku-4.5 LLM-judge classifying each Stage-2 candidate as close-duplicate vs. same-topic-neighbor; only Stage-3 close-duplicate records are removed from the training pool. Threshold sensitivity reported in Table 5 of the paper. Construction-audit lineage: 14,294 pre-audit -> 7,236 surviving the construction audit (the v1 candidate). A subsequent joint re-audit pass at the same thresholds against PhysReason-full and PhysUniBench-en (which were not in the construction-audit set) dropped a further 804 records (PhysReason-full 540, PhysUniBench-en 186 dominant) -> 6,432 PhysCorp-A v2 surviving, 0/0 by construction at the joint operating thresholds against all six paper-canonical evals.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. Problems are physics olympiad / textbook content with no PII.
rai:syntheticDataDescription: Limited and disclosed. The training-corpus distributions (physcorp_pre_audit, physcorp_a, physr1corp) include the 1,293-record MMMU+o1-CoT seed (Appendix A of the paper) whose chain-of-thought solutions were generated by Claude Sonnet 4.5 over MMMU base problems (MIT-licensed); the underlying problem statements are not synthetic. In addition, the eight-field annotation schema (concept, difficulty, problem-type, expected-solution-length, math-level, modality, language, license) was Sonnet-4.5-batch-annotated for ~3,900 records across all distributions; gold answers and problem statements are not synthetic. The held-out evaluation distribution (physolym_a) contains no synthetic problem content: all 500 problems and reference solutions are real physics-olympiad material from the Estonian Physics Olympiad, Kevin Zhou's olympiad handouts, and seven international olympiads (IPhO, NBPhO, EuPhO, APhO, USAPhO, INPhO, IYPT); only the metadata-schema annotation fields on physolym_a records are LLM-generated. No fully-synthetic problems, no synthetic images, and no model-rewritten gold answers ship in any released distribution.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Quarterly contamination audit against any newly-released physics-VL benchmark. Source code, training launchers, and eval harness maintained at https://github.com/shanyang-me/physics-r1-neurips2026.
rai:dataLimitations: Stage-1 audit does not catch arbitrary paraphrase or translation; Stage-2 does not catch numerical-value substitution. Both are reported as floors, not ceilings. The 13.9% LLM-judge unjudgeable rate on PhysOlym-A is documented. MMMU-Pro Physics is excluded from the paper's headline table because the audit found 52/60 (86.7%) of its records flagged under the joint Stage-1 OR Stage-2 thresholds against the candidate PhysR1Corp pool; downstream users training on this corpus or its parents should not report MMMU-Pro Physics scores.
rai:dataBiases: Three documented distributional biases in PhysOlym-A and the training corpus. (1) Cross-lingual quality asymmetry: on the 59-problem Estonian/English bilingual subset of PhysOlym-A, Claude Sonnet 4.5 attains 30.5% strict on Estonian originals vs. 13.6% on English translations of identical problems (sign test on 16 discordant pairs p=0.011, McNemar exact p=0.021, paired bootstrap 95% CI [+5.1, +28.9] pp). The English versions are translations from Estonian-first composition; physics vocabulary, grammatical-case mapping, and scope subtleties degrade information content. Direction-of-effect is observed on Sonnet 4.5 specifically and may flip for low-resource-language-weak open-source models. (2) Per-physics-category accuracy variance: on OlympiadBench-Physics under identical Sonnet 4.5 evaluation, electromagnetism (n=237, 38.4%) and astrophysics (n=70, 72.9%) span a 34-pp same-model gap. Models trained on the corpus inherit this category imbalance unless rebalanced. (3) Difficulty-stratified accuracy: on the 131 Estonian PhO problems carrying organizer-issued 1-10 difficulty, Sonnet decays from 62.5% at difficulty 1 to a hard 0% floor at difficulties 3, 6, 8, and 10; downstream evaluations should report difficulty-stratified accuracy rather than aggregate score where the underlying difficulty distribution differs.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Intended use: research on visual physics reasoning, contamination-audit methodology, cross-lingual evaluation of LLMs, and RL post-training of vision-language models. PhysOlym-A is intended as held-out evaluation; we explicitly request that the community treat it as test-only and not include it in pretraining or fine-tuning corpora. Out-of-scope: the corpus is not a calibrated test of general physics ability outside the visual physics-reasoning context, not a substitute for human olympiad grading, and not a measure of physics research capability. The dense five-component reward and Physics-R1 reference recipe are released to support reproducibility, not to argue a method contribution; component weights are not tuned to maximize any task-specific score and should not be used as a gold standard. The contamination-audit pipeline is released as a measurement tool, not a certification authority; the threshold-sensitivity disclosure (Table 5 of the paper) is intended to prevent over-claims of 'contamination-free' status by downstream users.
rai:dataUseCases: Visual physics reasoning research, olympiad-level evaluation, contamination-audit methodology development, cross-lingual evaluation studies, RL post-training of vision-language models.
